meta.title: Orbital — country startup-ecosystem directories
meta.note: Human-curated profiles of national startup ecosystems for founders considering relocation. Rather than re-collecting every ecosystem detail itself, Orbital points OUT to existing, well-known country-specific startup directories/portals via `directories`. Each entry is a stand-alone record keyed by `slug` so it can later be served from a cloud database without code changes (see src/data/countries.ts for the swap point). Links and visa details change frequently — always confirm on the official source. Each profile shows a round flag icon collected by scripts/fetch-flags.ts into public/flags/ and indexed by src/lib/flagManifest.json (resolved via flagSrc()); add a new country's ISO code to that script when extending this list.

schema.name: Display name; MUST match the `country` value used in the program datasets so program counts join
schema.summary: 1-2 sentence ecosystem overview for a relocating founder
schema.highlights: Short bullet facts (strengths, hubs, notable wins)
schema.visas: Founder/startup-relevant visa or residency routes: { name, description, url }
schema.organizations: Key ecosystem orgs to know: { name, type, description, url }
schema.links: Other useful official/portal links: { label, url }
schema.directories: External, country-specific startup-ecosystem directories/portals Orbital points founders to: { label, url, description? }
schema.updatedAt: ISO date this record was last verified
schema.source: Where the record was curated from (free text)

countries.name: USA
countries.summary: The deepest pool of startup capital, talent and programs on earth, anchored by the SF Bay Area and New York. Unmatched for raising venture funding, but immigration is the main friction for foreign founders.
highlights: Largest venture capital market globally; SF Bay Area + NYC dominate.
Most accelerators and residencies (YC, Techstars, a16z Speedrun, f.inc).
No dedicated startup visa — founders typically use O-1, H-1B, or the International Entrepreneur Parole route.

visas.name: International Entrepreneur Parole (IEP)
visas.description: Up to 5 years for founders of fast-growing startups with qualified investment.

visas.name: O-1A Visa
visas.description: For individuals with extraordinary ability — commonly used by founders.

organizations.name: U.S. Small Business Administration (SBA)
organizations.description: Federal agency supporting small businesses and startups.

organizations.name: National Venture Capital Association
organizations.description: Trade association of the US VC industry.

directories.label: Crunchbase
directories.description: The largest open database of US (and global) startups, funding rounds and investors.

directories.label: Dealroom — United States
directories.description: Data-driven profile of the US tech ecosystem: unicorns, funding and top companies.

countries.name: UK
countries.summary: Europe's largest startup hub centred on London, with strong fintech, AI and deep-tech scenes and a dedicated, well-understood founder visa route.
highlights: London is Europe's leading VC and fintech hub.
Innovator Founder visa is purpose-built for startup founders.
Strong university spinout pipeline (Oxford, Cambridge, Imperial).

visas.name: Innovator Founder visa
visas.description: For founders with an innovative, viable and scalable business endorsed by an approved body.

visas.name: Global Talent visa
visas.description: For leaders or potential leaders in tech, research and the arts.

organizations.name: Tech Nation / Innovate UK
organizations.description: National support and endorsement for high-growth tech companies.

organizations.name: British Business Bank
organizations.description: Government-owned development bank improving access to finance for smaller businesses.

directories.label: Dealroom — United Kingdom
directories.description: Data-driven profile of the UK tech ecosystem: funding, unicorns and top startups.

directories.label: Tech Nation
directories.description: National growth platform and data hub for UK tech companies.

countries.name: Singapore
countries.summary: The gateway to Southeast Asia: pro-business regulation, deep government grants, and a fast, founder-friendly visa make it the default Asian HQ for many startups.
highlights: Strong government co-investment and grant ecosystem (Startup SG).
Hub for Southeast Asian expansion and fintech.
Fast company incorporation and clear tax regime.

visas.name: EntrePass
visas.description: For eligible foreign entrepreneurs starting and operating a venture-backed or innovative business.

organizations.name: Enterprise Singapore
organizations.description: Government agency championing enterprise development, including Startup SG.

organizations.name: Startup SG
organizations.description: National umbrella for startup grants, talent and funding schemes.

directories.label: Startup SG
directories.description: Singapore's national startup portal and directory of programmes, grants and partners.

directories.label: Dealroom — Singapore
directories.description: Data-driven profile of Singapore's tech ecosystem.

countries.name: UAE
countries.summary: Dubai and Abu Dhabi have built fast-growing, capital-rich ecosystems with zero personal income tax, long-term 'Golden' residency, and aggressive sovereign-backed funding.
highlights: No personal income tax; multiple free zones for foreign ownership.
Sovereign and government-backed funds actively deploying capital.
Long-term residency via the Golden Visa for entrepreneurs and talent.

visas.name: Golden Visa (Entrepreneurs)
visas.description: Long-term (up to 10-year) residency for entrepreneurs and investors.

organizations.name: Hub71 (Abu Dhabi)
organizations.description: Global tech ecosystem offering incentives, capital and market access.

organizations.name: Dubai SME
organizations.description: Agency of the Dubai Economy supporting entrepreneurship and SMEs.

directories.label: Dealroom — United Arab Emirates
directories.description: Data-driven profile of the UAE tech ecosystem.

countries.name: Estonia
countries.summary: A small but outsized digital-first ecosystem: e-Residency lets founders run an EU company fully online, and a dedicated Startup Visa welcomes non-EU founders.
highlights: e-Residency: run an EU-based company entirely online.
Highest number of unicorns per capita in Europe (Wise, Bolt, Skype heritage).
Dedicated Startup Visa and Startup Estonia programme.

visas.name: Estonian Startup Visa
visas.description: For non-EU founders to relocate and build, or to hire from abroad.

visas.name: e-Residency
visas.description: Digital identity to register and run an EU company remotely (not a residence permit).

organizations.name: Startup Estonia
organizations.description: Government initiative to supercharge the local startup ecosystem.

directories.label: Startup Estonia — startup database
directories.description: Official directory of Estonian startups maintained by Startup Estonia.

countries.name: France
countries.summary: A heavily state-supported ecosystem centred on Paris and Station F, with the well-known French Tech Visa and generous public co-funding via Bpifrance.
highlights: Station F (Paris) is one of the world's largest startup campuses.
Strong public funding and R&D tax credits via Bpifrance.
French Tech Visa is a fast track for founders, employees and investors.

visas.name: French Tech Visa for Founders
visas.description: 4-year renewable residence permit for founders selected by a partner incubator/accelerator.

organizations.name: La French Tech
organizations.description: National movement and support network for French startups.

organizations.name: Bpifrance
organizations.description: Public investment bank financing and supporting French companies.

directories.label: La French Tech
directories.description: France's national startup movement, support network and ecosystem map.

directories.label: Dealroom — France
directories.description: Data-driven profile of the French tech ecosystem.

countries.name: Germany
countries.summary: Europe's largest economy with a deep startup scene centred on Berlin and Munich, strong in B2B software, mobility and deep tech, backed by substantial public and corporate funding.
highlights: Berlin is one of Europe's top startup hubs; Munich leads in deep tech.
Strong corporate and public co-investment (e.g. KfW, High-Tech Gründerfonds).
Large domestic and EU market on the doorstep.

directories.label: Dealroom — Germany
directories.description: Data-driven profile of the German tech ecosystem: funding, unicorns and top startups.

directories.label: Startupdetector
directories.description: Tracker and directory of newly registered German startups and funding rounds.

countries.name: Canada
countries.summary: A welcoming, immigration-friendly ecosystem with strong AI hubs in Toronto, Montreal and Vancouver, plus a dedicated federal Start-up Visa that grants permanent residency.
highlights: Start-up Visa offers a direct route to permanent residency for founders.
World-class AI research clusters in Toronto and Montreal.
Generous SR&ED R&D tax incentives.

visas.name: Start-up Visa Program
visas.description: Permanent residency for entrepreneurs with a qualifying business and support from a designated organization.

directories.label: Dealroom — Canada
directories.description: Data-driven profile of the Canadian tech ecosystem.

countries.name: Australia
countries.summary: A maturing ecosystem led by Sydney and Melbourne with notable global exits (Atlassian, Canva, SafetyCulture) and a growing base of accelerators and venture capital.
highlights: Major global successes: Atlassian, Canva, Airwallex.
Sydney and Melbourne are the primary hubs.
Strong APAC time-zone position and English-language market.

directories.label: Techboard
directories.description: Australia's largest directory and funding-data source for startup and tech companies.

directories.label: Dealroom — Australia
directories.description: Data-driven profile of the Australian tech ecosystem.

countries.name: Austria
countries.summary: A compact, well-funded ecosystem centred on Vienna, strong in deep tech and B2B software, with active public support via the Austria Wirtschaftsservice (aws).
highlights: Vienna is the primary hub and a gateway to Central/Eastern Europe.
Strong public grant support through aws and FFG.
Notable scaleups including Bitpanda and GoStudent.

directories.label: Dealroom — Austria
directories.description: Data-driven profile of the Austrian tech ecosystem.

countries.name: Finland
countries.summary: A small but globally connected ecosystem centred on Helsinki, famous for Slush and a strong gaming and deep-tech scene, with active public support via Business Finland.
highlights: Home of Slush, the world's leading founder-focused startup event.
Strong gaming heritage (Supercell, Rovio) and deep-tech research.
Active public funding via Business Finland.

visas.name: Startup permit
visas.description: Residence permit for non-EU founders building an innovative startup in Finland, with a Business Finland eligibility statement.

organizations.name: Business Finland
organizations.description: Public funding and internationalization agency supporting Finnish startups.

directories.label: Slush
directories.description: Founder-focused community and event run by the not-for-profit Startup Foundation, anchoring the Finnish ecosystem.

directories.label: Dealroom — Finland
directories.description: Data-driven profile of the Finnish tech ecosystem.

countries.name: Sweden
countries.summary: A highly productive ecosystem centred on Stockholm, which produces an outsized number of unicorns (Spotify, Klarna, Northvolt) per capita.
highlights: Stockholm is one of Europe's most prolific unicorn factories.
Major successes: Spotify, Klarna, Mojang, Northvolt.
Strong design, fintech and climate-tech scenes.

directories.label: Dealroom — Sweden
directories.description: Data-driven profile of the Swedish tech ecosystem.

countries.name: Switzerland
countries.summary: A research-rich, capital-dense ecosystem anchored by ETH Zürich and EPFL, strong in deep tech, biotech, robotics and crypto (the Zug 'Crypto Valley').
highlights: ETH Zürich and EPFL drive world-class deep-tech spinouts.
Zug's 'Crypto Valley' is a global blockchain hub.
Stable, high-trust environment with strong access to capital.

directories.label: startup.ch (IFJ)
directories.description: Swiss startup support platform and resource directory run by the IFJ Institut für Jungunternehmen.

directories.label: Dealroom — Switzerland
directories.description: Data-driven profile of the Swiss tech ecosystem.

countries.name: India
countries.summary: One of the world's largest and fastest-growing startup ecosystems, spanning Bengaluru, Delhi NCR and Mumbai, with a vast domestic market and strong government backing via Startup India.
highlights: Third-largest startup ecosystem globally by number of startups.
Major hubs: Bengaluru, Delhi NCR, Mumbai, Hyderabad.
National Startup India programme offers recognition, tax benefits and funding.

organizations.name: Startup India
organizations.description: Flagship national initiative supporting startup recognition, funding and networking.

directories.label: Startup India
directories.description: Official Government of India portal and directory of recognised startups, schemes and investors.

directories.label: Dealroom — India
directories.description: Data-driven profile of the Indian tech ecosystem.

countries.name: Indonesia
countries.summary: Southeast Asia's largest economy and consumer market, with a fast-growing Jakarta-centred ecosystem that has produced decacorns like GoTo and unicorns across fintech and e-commerce.
highlights: Largest population and digital consumer market in Southeast Asia.
Major successes: GoTo (Gojek + Tokopedia), Traveloka, Bukalapak.
Jakarta is the dominant hub.

directories.label: Dealroom — Indonesia
directories.description: Data-driven profile of the Indonesian tech ecosystem.

countries.name: Malaysia
countries.summary: A growing Southeast Asian ecosystem centred on Kuala Lumpur, with strong government backing via MaGIC/Cradle and a strategic position between Singapore and the wider region.
highlights: Kuala Lumpur is the primary hub, supported by national agencies.
Government support via Cradle Fund and MyStartup.
Cost-effective base for Southeast Asian expansion.

directories.label: Dealroom — Malaysia
directories.description: Data-driven profile of the Malaysian tech ecosystem.

countries.name: Vietnam
countries.summary: A young, fast-rising ecosystem centred on Ho Chi Minh City and Hanoi, strong in fintech and gaming, with a large engineering-talent pool and rising venture interest.
highlights: Ho Chi Minh City and Hanoi are the twin hubs.
Strong, low-cost engineering talent base.
Notable successes include VNG, MoMo and Sky Mavis.

directories.label: Dealroom — Vietnam
directories.description: Data-driven profile of the Vietnamese tech ecosystem.

countries.name: Mexico
countries.summary: Latin America's second-largest ecosystem, anchored by Mexico City and Monterrey, strong in fintech and e-commerce and benefiting from proximity to the US market.
highlights: Mexico City and Monterrey are the leading hubs.
Major fintech successes including Clip, Konfío and Bitso.
Proximity and trade ties to the US (USMCA).

directories.label: Dealroom — Mexico
directories.description: Data-driven profile of the Mexican tech ecosystem.

countries.name: Colombia
countries.summary: A rising Latin American ecosystem centred on Bogotá and Medellín, strong in fintech and proptech, with national support via iNNpulsa Colombia.
highlights: Bogotá and Medellín are the primary hubs.
Home to Rappi, one of Latin America's largest startups.
National entrepreneurship support via iNNpulsa Colombia.

directories.label: Dealroom — Colombia
directories.description: Data-driven profile of the Colombian tech ecosystem.

countries.name: Argentina
countries.summary: A talent-rich ecosystem centred on Buenos Aires that has produced an outsized number of unicorns (MercadoLibre, Globant, Auth0, Ualá) despite macroeconomic volatility.
highlights: Buenos Aires is the dominant hub and a Latin American talent magnet.
Produced unicorns: MercadoLibre, Globant, Auth0, Ualá, OLX.
Strong, globally competitive engineering talent.

directories.label: Dealroom — Argentina
directories.description: Data-driven profile of the Argentine tech ecosystem.
